summary.narrative: Matt Meyer, 76th Governor of Delaware, is a former public school math teacher and small business owner who served as New Castle County Executive (2017-2025). His campaign platform emphasized universal pre-K, universal free school meals, medical debt cancellation, police accountability, and abortion rights constitutional amendment. Early tenure focused on affordable housing and permitting streamlining.
summary.grade_rationale: Early action on affordable housing via executive order demonstrates commitment; limited time in office to assess major legislative achievements; medical debt, pre-K, school meals require legislative passage or budget allocation not yet confirmed

promises.normalized: Establish universal free school meals for all students
Cancel medical debt and establish debt relief programs
Enshrine abortion rights in state constitution via amendment

data_gaps: Specific budget allocations for campaign promises not disclosed
Legislative timelines and co-sponsor commitments unknown
Detailed metrics for police accountability reforms undefined
County-level accomplishments as NCC Executive not fully itemized
Performance data on first 100 days initiatives incomplete
Medical debt cancellation mechanism and scope unclear
Election margin percentage not publicly available
